explanations.7: This tells Docker to use the 'development' stage from your Dockerfile instead of the default final stage, which lets you include extra tools for coding.
explanations.21: This sets which user account the container runs as (the numbers are user ID and group ID); it's using the variables you defined above with fallback values if they're not set.
explanations.27: The dot (.) means your current folder on your computer gets mapped into the container at the path stored in DOCKER_APP_HOME, so changes you make locally appear instantly in the container.
explanations.29: This creates a persistent storage space just for VSCode extensions so they survive container restarts and you don't have to reinstall them each time.
explanations.31: This stores your bash command history in a Docker volume so you keep your terminal history even after the container stops and restarts.
